Professor View 01 / 27
Travel English -- 48 Aulas

Marcos Mansour

Valuation & International Leadership

B1 (Intermediate) São Paulo (SP) Appraisal Engineer & Partner Online
Progresso Geral0%
Velocidade:
Nome

Marcos Mansour

Idade

42 anos

Cidade

São Paulo, SP (Vila Romana)

Profissão

Engenheiro Avaliador / Perito — sócio (32 funcionários)

Nível

B1 (Intermediário) — compreensão alta, produção em desenvolvimento

Foco

Professional English: valuation, congressos, networking internacional, fala espontânea

Frequência

Aulas online, semanais

Total de Aulas

18 aulas

Personagem-Jornada

De: Um líder da área de valuation que entende ~95% do inglês, mas trava ao falar sob pressão — o espanhol sobrepõe o inglês na hora de produzir, e ele omite informações quando não encontra a palavra.

Para: Um comunicador confiante que se apresenta, faz networking, conduz sessões e firma relações com colegas dos EUA e Canadá no congresso de Washington — sem intermediários. Em 18 aulas.

Promessa Transformadora

Nas palavras do Marcos: "O mais importante para mim é conseguir me comunicar e entender." Em 18 aulas, ele vai deixar de travar e simplificar para conseguir sustentar conversas reais em inglês — apresentar-se, apresentar pessoas, circular com autonomia e construir as relações internacionais que sustentam sua candidatura à presidência da UPAV.

Forças
  • Compreensão auditiva alta (entende ~95% sem pedir repetição)
  • Metacognição avançada: sabe onde trava e por quê
  • Prova de que adquire língua quando o contexto é real (espanhol em 2 anos)
  • Vocabulário técnico já parcialmente ativo (USPAP, appraisal, IVS)
  • Motivação concreta e urgente: Washington (novembro) + meta UPAV 2029
Pontos de Melhoria
  • Produção oral fragmentada, frases soltas sem conectivos
  • Interferência do espanhol bloqueia o acesso lexical em inglês
  • Bloqueio de vocabulário básico na hora de falar
  • Falta de estratégias para contornar lacunas (dizer de outra forma)
  • Aversão à exposição pública ao erro (não quer "passar vergonha")

Currículo Completo — 18 Aulas

#TemaFoco LinguísticoAtividade PrincipalStatus
1Introducing Yourself ProfessionallyPresent simple (fatos/rotina) + WH-questionsApresentar-se a um colega estrangeiro no congressoAtual
2Your Career JourneyPast simple, marcadores de tempoContar sua trajetória profissionalPróxima
3Your Field — Valuation & AppraisalVocabulário técnico, explicar processosExplicar o que é valuation para um leigoPróxima
4Small Talk & Networking at EventsPerguntas abertas, conectivos de conversaIniciar e manter uma conversa informalPróxima
5Introducing & Presenting Other PeopleThis is.../ Have you met...?, terceira pessoaApresentar um colega a outra pessoaPróxima
6Asking Questions in Sessions (Q&A)Perguntas indiretas, polidezFazer perguntas numa sessão do congressoPróxima
7Answering Questions About Your WorkClarificar, parafrasear, ganhar tempoResponder perguntas sobre o seu trabalhoPróxima
8Giving a Short Professional PresentationSignposting (first/then/finally)Apresentação curta estruturadaPróxima
9Trends — AI in ValuationDescrever tendências, present continuousFalar sobre IA aplicada à avaliaçãoPróxima
10Meetings & the UPAV CommitteeLinguagem de reunião, sugestõesParticipar de uma reunião de comissãoPróxima
11At the Airport & ImmigrationPerguntas e respostas de viagemPassar pela imigração nos EUAPróxima
12Hotel Check-in & Getting HelpPedidos educados, can/couldFazer check-in e resolver problemasPróxima
13Eating Out & OrderingWould like, fazer pedidosPedir em um restaurantePróxima
14Getting Around WashingtonDireções, imperativosPedir e seguir direções na cidadePróxima
15Building Relationships — Social Dinners & InvitationsConvites, aceitar/recusarConduzir um jantar social de networkingPróxima
16Talking About Goals & the FutureWill / going to, planosFalar de objetivos (presidência UPAV)Próxima
17Negotiating & Building PartnershipsCondicionais, linguagem de acordoNegociar uma parceria institucionalPróxima
18The Confident Communicator (Review & Integration)Consolidação de todas as estruturasSimulação completa do congressoPróxima

Metodologia

1. Produção Acima de Tudo

Marcos entende muito mais do que produz. Cada aula prioriza a fala (55%): role-plays, apresentações e produção livre para fechar o gap entre compreensão e produção.

2. Estratégias de Contorno

Ensinar a "dizer de outra forma" quando a palavra não vem — exatamente a tática que ele já domina no espanhol e quer transferir para o inglês.

3. Contexto Real e Urgente

Todo conteúdo usa cenários reais: congresso de Washington, comissão UPAV, networking com EUA e Canadá. Marcos pratica o que vai fazer de verdade.

4. Espanhol como Acelerador (com cuidado)

Aproveitar cognatos (valuation/valoración) e monitorar a interferência ativa do espanhol que bloqueia o acesso ao inglês na produção.

5. Ambiente Seguro, Erro sem Vergonha

Feedback adiado (delayed correction) durante a fala livre. Marcos tem aversão à exposição ao erro; o erro vira aprendizado, nunca constrangimento.

Mapa de Personalidade

IntrovertidoExtrovertido
Aprendiz visualAprendiz auditivo
DetalhistaBig picture
Preferência por regrasPreferência por prática
Ritmo lentoRitmo acelerado / dinâmico
Evita riscosAceita desafios
Foco acadêmicoFoco profissional
Aula 01 -- Pre-class

Introducing Yourself Professionally

Meeting a colleague at an international congress. Key words: role, company, field, in charge of, based in, colleague, partner, industry, networking, get in touch. Structures: present simple for facts and routines + WH-questions.
0%
▼

Stage 1.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para escutar.

Role -- cargo, função (the job or position a person has)
"My role is to lead the valuation team."
Company -- empresa (an organization that sells products or services)
"I am a partner in a valuation company."
Field -- área de atuação (the area of work you specialize in)
"I work in the field of property valuation."
In charge of -- responsável por (responsible for something or someone)
"I am in charge of the appraisal department."
Based in -- localizado em (located in a particular place)
"Our company is based in São Paulo."
Colleague -- colega de trabalho (a person you work with)
"This is my colleague from the technical team."
Partner -- sócio (a co-owner of a company)
"I am a partner in a company with thirty-two employees."
Industry -- setor, ramo (a specific type of business activity)
"I work in the real estate and valuation industry."
Networking -- criar contatos profissionais (meeting people to build professional contacts)
"Networking at congresses helps me find new partners."
Get in touch -- entrar em contato (to make contact or communicate with someone)
"Let's get in touch after the congress."

Stage 1.2: Matching

Practice

Relacione cada palavra com a definição correta.

Role
Company
Field
In charge of
Based in
Colleague
Partner
Industry
Networking
Get in touch

Stage 1.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas.

Daniel works as an appraisal engineer at an international congress. He is a partner in his own company, which is based in Mexico City. His company works in the valuation industry, and he is in charge of the technical team. "I work in the field of property and asset valuation," he explains to a new colleague. "My role involves leading projects and meeting clients." Daniel loves networking at congresses because it helps him build new connections. When he meets someone interesting, he always says: "Let's get in touch after the event." His colleague asks: "What do you do? Where are you based?" and Daniel answers with confidence.

1. Por que dizemos "Daniel works" com -s no final?
A Porque a ação aconteceu no passado.
B Porque, no present simple, he/she/it leva -s no verbo.
C Porque "Daniel" é plural.
2. "His company is based in Mexico City" usa o present simple para expressar:
A Um fato permanente (onde a empresa fica localizada).
B Uma ação que vai acontecer no futuro.
C Algo que aconteceu só uma vez ontem.
3. Which question does Daniel's colleague ask correctly?
A "What you do?"
B "What do you do? Where are you based?"
C "Where you based are?"

Stage 1.4: Grammar Tip -- Present Simple & WH-Questions

GRAMMAR

Quando usar o present simple para se apresentar (explicação em inglês e português).

FormUse / UsoExample
I / You / We + verbFatos e rotinas (sem -s)I work in valuation.
He / She / It + verb+s3ª pessoa SEMPRE leva -s (cuidado: o espanhol não tem isso)She leads the team.
am / is / areIdentidade, cargo, lugar. Use a/an antes da profissãoI am an engineer.
WH + do + you + verbPerguntar sobre alguém (verbo de ação)What do you do?
WH + are + youCom to be, o verbo vem antes do sujeito: Where are you based? / What field are you in?

Stage 1.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com a palavra correta. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase inteira.

"I as an appraisal engineer."
"My colleague in the technical team."
"I am the valuation team."
"Our company is São Paulo."
"What you do at the congress?"
"Let's after the congress."

Stage 2: Put the Introduction in Order

Order

Coloque os passos de uma apresentação profissional num congresso na ordem correta.

?Say your company and your field.
?Suggest staying in touch and exchange cards.
?Greet the person and say "Nice to meet you."
?Ask the other person a question (What do you do?).
?Say your name and your role.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e depois grave você mesmo dizendo-a.

Hi, I am Marcos Mansour. Nice to meet you.
Oi, eu sou o Marcos Mansour. Prazer em conhecer você.
I work as an appraisal engineer.
Eu trabalho como engenheiro avaliador.
I am a partner, and I am in charge of the valuation team.
Eu sou sócio e sou responsável pela equipe de avaliação.
Our company is based in São Paulo, Brazil.
Nossa empresa fica em São Paulo, Brasil.
What do you do? Where are you based?
O que você faz? Onde você fica baseado?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação profissional.

A colleague at the congress asks you to describe your work. What do you say?
A "I do appraisal things."
B "I work as an appraisal engineer. I am a partner and I am in charge of the valuation team."
C "I am engineer, I make values."
Someone asks: "Where are you based?" You respond:
A "I am based São Paulo."
B "My company is based in São Paulo, Brazil."
C "I based in São Paulo am."
You want to ask a new colleague about their job. The correct question is:
A "What you do for work?"
B "Which field you are in?"
C "What do you do? Which field are you in?"
You want to end the conversation and keep the contact. You say:
A "It was great meeting you. Let's get in touch after the congress."
B "Okay, bye, finish now."
C "We get in touch since the congress."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesmo respondendo à pergunta abaixo. Não há resposta certa ou errada.

Imagine you are at the International Valuation Congress in Washington. A colleague from the United States walks up to you. Introduce yourself: your name, your role, your company, your field, and why you are here. Use present simple. Take your time.

Survival Card -- Lesson 1

1Hi, I'm Marcos Mansour. Nice to meet you.Oi, sou o Marcos Mansour. Prazer.
2I work as an appraisal engineer.Eu trabalho como engenheiro avaliador.
3I am a partner, and I am in charge of the valuation team.Sou sócio e responsável pela equipe de avaliação.
4Our company is based in São Paulo, Brazil.Nossa empresa fica em São Paulo, Brasil.
5It was great meeting you. Let's get in touch.Foi ótimo te conhecer. Vamos manter contato.
Aula 02 -- Pre-class

Your Career Journey

Telling the story of your professional path. Key words: graduate, degree, hire, found, promote, experience, achievement, client, milestone, career path. Structures: past simple (regular + irregular) + time markers.
0%
▼

Stage 2.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para escutar.

Graduate -- formar-se (to finish your studies at a university)
"I graduated as an engineer in 2004."
Degree -- diploma, formação (a qualification from a university)
"I have a degree in civil engineering."
Hire -- contratar (to give someone a job)
"A large firm hired me as a junior appraiser."
Found -- fundar, abrir (to start a company; past: founded)
"I founded my own valuation firm in 2015."
Promote -- promover (to move someone to a higher position)
"After three years, they promoted me to team leader."
Experience -- experiência (knowledge gained over years of work)
"I have over twenty years of experience in valuation."
Achievement -- conquista, realização (something important you did successfully)
"Founding my own firm was my biggest achievement."
Client -- cliente (a person or company that pays you for your work)
"My first client was a small construction company."
Milestone -- marco (an important point that marks progress)
"Winning that contract was a real milestone."
Career path -- trajetória profissional (the series of jobs in your working life)
"My career path took me from site work to leadership."

Stage 2.2: Matching

Practice

Relacione cada palavra com a definição correta.

Graduate
Degree
Hire
Found
Promote
Experience
Achievement
Client
Milestone
Career path

Stage 2.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas.

Sofia studied economics in Toronto and graduated in 2006. After university, a bank hired her as a financial analyst. She worked there for five years and gained a lot of experience. Then, ten years ago, she moved into valuation. In 2018, she became a senior partner at her firm. "Winning my first international client was a real milestone," she says. When a colleague asked, "How did you get into this field?", Sofia answered: "I started in finance, and step by step, I built my career path."

1. Por que dizemos "she became" e não "she becomed"?
A Porque "become" é um verbo regular.
B Porque "become" é irregular: o passado é "became".
C Porque a ação acontece no presente.
2. Na pergunta "How did you get into this field?", por que usamos "get" e não "got"?
A Porque, depois de "did", usamos o verbo na forma base.
B Porque a pergunta é sobre o futuro.
C Porque "get" nunca muda de forma.
3. Which sentence correctly uses a time marker with the past simple?
A "She graduate on 2006."
B "She graduated in 2006."
C "She is graduated since 2006."

Stage 2.4: Grammar Tip -- Past Simple & Time Markers

GRAMMAR

Quando e como usar o past simple para contar sua trajetória (explicação em inglês e português).

FormUse / UsoExample
verb + -ed (regular)Ações terminadas no passadoI studied. They hired me.
irregular verbsDecorar (não seguem regra). Cuidado com o espanhol!become → became, get → got, begin → began
did + verbo basePerguntas e negativasDid you graduate? I did not start there.
Time markersQuando aconteceu: in 2004, then, later, after that, two years ago (use in com anos, não "on")

Stage 2.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com o verbo correto no passado. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase inteira.

"I civil engineering in São Paulo."
"A firm me as a junior appraiser."
"In 2015, I my own company."
"Soon after, I a partner."
"When you start your career?"
"Winning that contract a real milestone."

Stage 2: Put the Career Story in Order

Order

Coloque os passos de uma trajetória de carreira na ordem cronológica correta.

?A firm hired me and I gained experience.
?I founded my own company and became a partner.
?I studied engineering at university.
?They promoted me to team leader.
?I graduated in 2004.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e depois grave você mesmo dizendo-a.

I studied civil engineering and graduated in 2004.
Estudei engenharia civil e me formei em 2004.
A firm hired me as a junior appraiser.
Uma empresa me contratou como avaliador júnior.
In 2015, I founded my own firm and became a partner.
Em 2015, fundei minha própria empresa e me tornei sócio.
It took years of experience, but it was worth it.
Levou anos de experiência, mas valeu a pena.
When did you graduate? How did you get into your field?
Quando você se formou? Como você entrou na sua área?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação profissional.

A colleague asks: "When did you graduate?" You answer:
A "I graduate on 2004."
B "I graduated as a civil engineer in 2004."
C "I am graduated since 2004."
Someone asks about your first job. The best answer is:
A "A large firm hired me as a junior appraiser, and later they promoted me."
B "I am hired in a firm before many years."
C "My first job is a junior appraiser."
You want to ask a colleague about their background. The correct question is:
A "How you did get into this field?"
B "How did you got into this field?"
C "How did you get into this field?"
You describe a key moment in your career. You say:
A "Founding my own firm in 2015 was my biggest milestone."
B "I am founding my firm since 2015 milestone."
C "My firm founded me in 2015."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesmo respondendo à pergunta abaixo. Não há resposta certa ou errada.

Tell the story of your career so far: where you studied, your first job, your promotions, when you founded your company, and your biggest milestone. Use the past simple and time markers. Take your time.

Survival Card -- Lesson 2

1I studied civil engineering and graduated in 2004.Estudei engenharia civil e me formei em 2004.
2A firm hired me as a junior appraiser.Uma empresa me contratou como avaliador júnior.
3After that, they promoted me to team leader.Depois disso, me promoveram a líder de equipe.
4In 2015, I founded my own firm and became a partner.Em 2015, fundei minha empresa e me tornei sócio.
5It took years of experience, but it was worth it.Levou anos de experiência, mas valeu a pena.
Aula 03 -- Pre-class

Your Field — Valuation & Appraisal

Explaining what you do to a non-expert. Key words: value, asset, property, estimate, inspection, market value, accurate, standard, reliable, figure. Structures: present simple for processes + sequencing words (first/then/after that/finally).
0%
▼

Stage 3.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para escutar.

Value -- avaliar, valorar (to decide how much something is worth)
"We value buildings, land, and machines."
Asset -- ativo, bem (something valuable a company or person owns)
"A building is an asset; so is a machine."
Property -- imóvel, propriedade (a building or piece of land)
"We value commercial and industrial property."
Estimate -- estimar (to make a careful, informed guess about an amount)
"We estimate the value based on the market."
Inspection -- vistoria, inspeção (a careful, official check of a property)
"The first step is an inspection of the site."
Market value -- valor de mercado (the price an asset would sell for today)
"We compare similar sales to find the market value."
Accurate -- preciso, exato (correct and exact, with no mistakes)
"An accurate value protects the client."
Standard -- norma, padrão (an official rule everyone must follow)
"We follow international valuation standards."
Reliable -- confiável (something you can trust and depend on)
"Banks need a reliable valuation before a loan."
Figure -- cifra, número (a specific number or amount)
"The final figure goes into the report."

Stage 3.2: Matching

Practice

Relacione cada palavra com a definição correta.

Value
Asset
Property
Estimate
Inspection
Market value
Accurate
Standard
Reliable
Figure

Stage 3.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas.

"In simple words, our firm values assets," Marcos explains to a new contact. "The process has four steps. First, we do an inspection of the property. Then we compare it with similar sales to find the market value. After that, we estimate a fair figure. Finally, we write a clear report for the client." He adds: "The value must be accurate and reliable, because banks use it before a loan. We always follow strict standards."

1. Por que dizemos "our firm values" com -s no final?
A Porque a ação está no passado.
B Porque "firm" é he/she/it (3ª pessoa) no present simple.
C Porque "values" é plural.
2. Quais palavras mostram a ORDEM dos passos do processo?
A "must, use, follow"
B "first, then, after that, finally"
C "assets, property, report"
3. Why does Marcos say "in simple words"?
A Because he forgot the technical terms.
B To explain his field clearly to a non-expert, without jargon.
C Because the contact does not speak English.

Stage 3.4: Grammar Tip -- Describing a Process

GRAMMAR

Como descrever um processo de forma clara (explicação em inglês e português).

ToolUse / UsoExample
we + verbo (present simple)Descrever um processo de rotinaWe inspect, we compare.
it / the value + verbo+s3ª pessoa leva -s (the value means...)The value means the price.
first / then / after that / finallyMostrar a ordem dos passosFirst... then... finally...
In simple words, ... / Basically, ...Explicar para um leigo, sem jargão: In simple words, we find a fair price for the asset.

Stage 3.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com a palavra correta. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase inteira.

", we inspect the property."
"Then we compare it to find the ."
"The value must be accurate and ."
"We follow international valuation ."
", we write a report for the client."
"In simple words, we assets."

Stage 2: Put the Process in Order

Order

Coloque os passos do processo de valuation na ordem correta.

?After that, we estimate the market value.
?First, we do an inspection of the property.
?Finally, we write a clear report for the client.
?Then we compare it with similar sales.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e depois grave você mesmo dizendo-a.

In simple words, we decide how much an asset is worth.
Em palavras simples, decidimos quanto vale um ativo.
First, we do an inspection of the property.
Primeiro, fazemos uma vistoria do imóvel.
Then we compare it with similar sales.
Depois, comparamos com vendas parecidas.
The value must be accurate and reliable.
O valor precisa ser preciso e confiável.
Finally, we write a clear report for the client.
Por fim, escrevemos um relatório claro para o cliente.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação profissional.

A non-expert asks: "What is valuation?" The best answer is:
A "We do USPAP-compliant DCF and NPV models."
B "In simple words, we decide how much an asset is worth."
C "It is a thing with values and numbers."
You want to describe the steps of your process. The best answer is:
A "First we inspect, then we compare, after that we estimate, finally we report."
B "We inspect compare estimate report." (no order words)
C "We are inspecting since the report."
Someone asks why your work matters. The best answer is:
A "The value mean nothing important."
B "The value must be accurate and reliable, because banks use it before a loan."
C "Banks uses the value sometimes maybe."
A friend asks what you do all day. You say:
A "We value property and assets, and we write reports for clients."
B "I value the things in the assets of property."
C "My job is values, since reports."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesmo respondendo à pergunta abaixo. Não há resposta certa ou errada.

Explain your field to a non-expert: what valuation is, what assets you value, and the four steps of the process. Use simple words and sequencing words (first, then, after that, finally). Take your time.

Survival Card -- Lesson 3

1In simple words, we decide how much an asset is worth.Em palavras simples, decidimos quanto vale um ativo.
2First, we do an inspection of the property.Primeiro, fazemos uma vistoria do imóvel.
3Then we compare it with similar sales.Depois, comparamos com vendas parecidas.
4The value must be accurate and reliable.O valor precisa ser preciso e confiável.
5Finally, we write a clear report for the client.Por fim, escrevemos um relatório claro para o cliente.
Aula 04 -- Pre-class

Small Talk & Networking at Events

Starting and keeping a conversation going. Key words: break the ice, small talk, host, venue, by the way, mention, in common, topic, enjoy, catch up. Structures: open questions (What/How/Why) vs closed questions + connectives (by the way / speaking of).
0%
▼

Stage 4.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para escutar.

Break the ice -- quebrar o gelo (to start a friendly conversation)
"A simple question is a great way to break the ice."
Small talk -- conversa casual (light, friendly conversation about easy topics)
"Small talk helps people feel comfortable."
Host -- anfitrião, organizador (the person or company organizing an event)
"The host welcomed everyone to the congress."
Venue -- local do evento (the place where an event is held)
"This venue is close to the hotel, which is convenient."
By the way -- a propósito (a phrase to introduce a new, related topic)
"By the way, are you staying for the dinner?"
Mention -- mencionar (to say or refer to something briefly)
"You mentioned you work in valuation, right?"
In common -- em comum (a shared interest or experience between people)
"We have a lot in common -- we both love travel."
Topic -- assunto, tópico (the subject you are talking about)
"The weather is always a safe topic for small talk."
Enjoy -- aproveitar, gostar (to take pleasure in something)
"Are you enjoying the congress so far?"
Catch up -- colocar a conversa em dia (to talk and share news after some time)
"Let's catch up later over coffee."

Stage 4.2: Matching

Practice

Relacione cada palavra com a definição correta.

Break the ice
Small talk
Host
Venue
By the way
Mention
In common
Topic
Enjoy
Catch up

Stage 4.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas.

At the coffee break, Ellen wants to break the ice. Instead of a closed question, she asks an open one: "What did you think of the morning session?" Marcos answers and adds a detail. Then he keeps it going: "By the way, where did you travel from?" Ellen replies, and Marcos connects topics: "You mentioned you are from Chicago -- how is the valuation market there?" They soon find they have a lot in common. "Speaking of tonight," Ellen says, "are you staying for the dinner? Let's catch up there."

1. Por que "What did you think...?" é melhor que "Did you like it?" no small talk?
A Porque é mais curta.
B Porque é uma open question e convida uma resposta mais longa.
C Porque usa o passado.
2. Quais palavras Marcos e Ellen usam para LIGAR um tópico ao outro?
A "host, venue, topic"
B "by the way, speaking of"
C "enjoy, catch up"
3. What makes the conversation feel natural and easy?
A They only answer yes or no.
B They find something they have in common and ask questions back.
C They stop talking after one question.

Stage 4.4: Grammar Tip -- Open Questions & Connectives

GRAMMAR

Como manter a conversa viva (explicação em inglês e português).

ToolUse / UsoExample
Closed question (Do/Is/Are...?)Resposta rápida sim/nãoDo you like it?
Open question (What/How/Why + do...?)Convida uma resposta real e longaWhat do you think of it?
By the way / Speaking of...Acrescentar ou ligar um novo tópicoBy the way, where are you from?
Really? / That's interestingMostrar interesse e devolver uma pergunta: Really? Tell me more.

Stage 4.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com a palavra correta. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase inteira.

" do you think of the talks?"
", are you staying for the dinner?"
"It sounds like we have a lot ."
"Are you the congress so far?"
"Let's at the dinner."
"You you work in valuation."

Stage 2: Put the Conversation in Order

Order

Coloque os passos de uma boa conversa de networking na ordem correta.

?Use "by the way" to add a new topic.
?Break the ice with an open question.
?Find something you have in common and arrange to catch up.
?Answer, add a detail, and bounce a question back.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e depois grave você mesmo dizendo-a.

What did you think of the morning session?
O que você achou da sessão da manhã?
By the way, where did you travel from?
A propósito, de onde você veio?
It sounds like we have a lot in common.
Parece que temos muito em comum.
Are you enjoying the congress so far?
Você está gostando do congresso até agora?
It was great talking to you. Let's catch up later.
Foi ótimo falar com você. Vamos nos falar mais tarde.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação de networking.

You want to break the ice at the coffee break. The best opener is:
A "Do you like it here?"
B "What did you think of the morning session?"
C "Who are you?"
The other person answers "Yes." You want to keep the conversation going. You say:
A "By the way, where did you travel from?"
B (say nothing and wait)
C "Okay, bye."
You discover you both work in valuation. The natural thing to say is:
A "We have much in common things."
B "It sounds like we have a lot in common!"
C "How do you call this?"
You want to end the chat but keep the contact warm. You say:
A "It was great talking to you. Let's catch up at the dinner."
B "I go now."
C "Finish. Goodbye."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesmo respondendo à pergunta abaixo. Não há resposta certa ou errada.

Imagine you are at the networking break. Start a conversation with a new contact: break the ice with an open question, keep it going with a connective (by the way / speaking of), find something in common, and arrange to catch up later. Speak for one minute.

Survival Card -- Lesson 4

1What did you think of the morning session?O que você achou da sessão da manhã?
2By the way, where did you travel from?A propósito, de onde você veio?
3It sounds like we have a lot in common.Parece que temos muito em comum.
4Are you enjoying the congress so far?Você está gostando do congresso até agora?
5It was great talking to you. Let's catch up later.Foi ótimo falar com você. Vamos nos falar mais tarde.
Aula 05 -- Pre-class

Introducing & Presenting Other People

Presenting one person to another. Key words: introduce, counterpart, mutual, acquaintance, connect, on behalf of, pleasure, get to know, lead, manage. Structures: This is... / Have you met...? + the third person -s (she leads, he manages).
0%
▼

Stage 5.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para escutar.

Introduce -- apresentar (to present one person to another)
"Let me introduce you to my colleague."
Counterpart -- equivalente, contraparte (a person with the same role in another company)
"I would like you to meet my counterpart from Chicago."
Mutual -- mútuo, em comum (shared by two or more people)
"We have a mutual friend in the industry."
Acquaintance -- conhecido (a person you know a little, not a close friend)
"He is a business acquaintance from last year's event."
Connect -- conectar, ligar pessoas (to bring two people together)
"Let me connect you two -- you have a lot in common."
On behalf of -- em nome de (representing or speaking for another person or group)
"On behalf of my firm, welcome to the congress."
Pleasure -- prazer (a feeling of happiness; used in polite introductions)
"It is a pleasure to meet you."
Get to know -- conhecer melhor (to learn more about a person over time)
"I would love to get to know your team better."
Lead -- liderar, chefiar (to be in charge of a team -- she leads)
"She leads the valuation team in Sao Paulo."
Manage -- gerenciar (to organize and be responsible for -- he manages)
"He manages our office in Chicago."

Stage 5.2: Matching

Practice

Relacione cada palavra com a definição correta.

Introduce
Counterpart
Mutual
Acquaintance
Connect
On behalf of
Pleasure
Get to know
Lead
Manage

Stage 5.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas.

At the congress, Marcos wants to introduce his colleague to a new contact. "This is Sarah," he says. "She leads our valuation team." Then he turns to the contact: "Have you met my counterpart from Chicago, David? He manages the office there." The contact smiles. "It is a pleasure. Actually, David is a mutual friend!" Marcos is happy to connect them. "On behalf of our team, welcome. I am sure you would love to get to know each other."

1. Por que dizemos "She leads" e "He manages" com -s no final?
A Porque é plural.
B Porque he/she (3ª pessoa) SEMPRE leva -s no present simple.
C Porque é uma pergunta.
2. Quais expressões Marcos usa para APRESENTAR alguém?
A "pleasure, mutual, connect"
B "This is... / Have you met...?"
C "on behalf of, counterpart"
3. Why does Marcos describe what Sarah and David do?
A To show he is busy.
B To help the contact understand each person's role.
C Because he forgot their names.

Stage 5.4: Grammar Tip -- Introducing & the Third Person -s

GRAMMAR

Como apresentar pessoas e falar de uma terceira pessoa (explicação em inglês e português).

ToolUse / UsoExample
This is + nomeApresentar uma pessoa diretamenteThis is Sarah.
Have you met + nome?Forma educada de apresentarHave you met John?
he / she + verbo+s3ª pessoa SEMPRE leva -s (cuidado: o espanhol não tem isso)She leads, he manages.
It is a pleasure to meet youResposta educada quando você é apresentado: A pleasure to meet you.

Stage 5.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com a palavra correta. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase inteira.

" Sarah, my colleague."
"She our valuation team."
"He our Chicago office."
"Have you my counterpart?"
"It is a to meet you."
" my firm, welcome."

Stage 2: Put the Introduction in Order

Order

Coloque os passos de uma boa apresentação na ordem correta.

?Say what each person does (she leads, he manages).
?Say "Have you met...?" or "This is...".
?Point out something they have in common and connect them.
?Give the person's name and role.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e depois grave você mesmo dizendo-a.

This is Sarah, my colleague.
Esta é a Sarah, minha colega.
She leads our valuation team.
Ela lidera a nossa equipe de avaliação.
Have you met my counterpart from Chicago?
Você já conheceu o meu equivalente de Chicago?
It is a pleasure to meet you.
É um prazer conhecer você.
On behalf of our team, thank you.
Em nome da nossa equipe, obrigado.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação de apresentação.

You want to introduce your colleague Sarah. You say:
A "This is Sarah. He leads the team."
B "This is Sarah. She leads the team."
C "This is Sarah. She lead the team."
You want to ask politely if two people already know each other:
A "Have you meet before?"
B "Have you met my counterpart from Chicago?"
C "You know him already, no?"
Someone is introduced to you. The most polite reply is:
A "It is a pleasure to meet you."
B "Okay, hello."
C "Yes, I know."
You welcome guests for your whole team. You say:
A "In behalf of my team, welcome."
B "On behalf of my team, welcome."
C "For my team behalf, welcome."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesmo respondendo à pergunta abaixo. Não há resposta certa ou errada.

Imagine you are at the congress with a colleague. Introduce your colleague to a new contact: use "This is..." or "Have you met...?", say what your colleague does (remember the -s: she leads / he manages), and point out something the two people have in common. Speak for one minute.

Survival Card -- Lesson 5

1This is Sarah, my colleague.Esta é a Sarah, minha colega.
2She leads our valuation team.Ela lidera a nossa equipe de avaliação.
3Have you met my counterpart from Chicago?Você já conheceu o meu equivalente de Chicago?
4It is a pleasure to meet you.É um prazer conhecer você.
5On behalf of our team, thank you.Em nome da nossa equipe, obrigado.
Aula 06 -- Pre-class

Asking Questions in Sessions (Q&A)

Asking polite questions in a session. Key words: session, audience, raise, clarify, follow-up, briefly, point, wonder, appreciate, concern. Structures: indirect questions (Could you tell me... / I was wondering if... / May I ask...) with subject before verb.
0%
▼

Stage 6.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para escutar.

Session -- sessão (a period of time for talks and discussion)
"The morning session ends with a short Q&A."
Audience -- plateia, público (the people who watch and listen to a talk)
"The audience had many questions for the speaker."
Raise -- levantar (a mão), trazer (um ponto) (to lift your hand or bring up a point)
"May I raise a quick point about your data?"
Clarify -- esclarecer (to make something clearer)
"Could you clarify what you mean by fair value?"
Follow-up -- pergunta de acompanhamento (a second question that builds on the first)
"May I ask a quick follow-up question?"
Briefly -- brevemente (in a short way, using few words)
"Could you briefly explain your method?"
Point -- ponto, argumento (an idea or argument you want to make)
"That is a very good point. Let me answer it."
Wonder -- querer saber, indagar (to think about and want to know something)
"I was wondering how you reached that figure."
Appreciate -- agradecer, valorizar (to be grateful or thankful)
"I appreciate your clear answer, thank you."
Concern -- preocupação (a worry or important issue)
"My main concern is the accuracy of the data."

Stage 6.2: Matching

Practice

Relacione cada palavra com a definição correta.

Session
Audience
Raise
Clarify
Follow-up
Briefly
Point
Wonder
Appreciate
Concern

Stage 6.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas.

After the talk, the host opens the floor: "Any questions?" Marcos raises his hand. Instead of a blunt question, he uses an indirect one: "May I ask what method you used?" The speaker answers. Then Marcos adds a follow-up: "I was wondering how you handled missing data." Finally, he asks to clarify a term: "Could you clarify what you mean by conservative?" Notice the word order in the second part -- subject before verb (where the data is, not "where is the data"). He ends politely: "I appreciate your clear answer."

1. Por que "Could you tell me where the data is?" é melhor que "Where is the data?"
A Porque é mais curta.
B Porque é uma indirect question e soa mais educada.
C Porque usa o passado.
2. Na indirect question, qual é a ordem das palavras na segunda parte?
A verbo antes do sujeito (where IS the data)
B sujeito antes do verbo (where the data IS)
C sempre com "do/did" no meio
3. What does Marcos do at the end of his question?
A He leaves without saying anything.
B He thanks the speaker: "I appreciate your clear answer."
C He asks the same question again.

Stage 6.4: Grammar Tip -- Indirect Questions

GRAMMAR

Como fazer perguntas educadas (explicação em inglês e português).

ToolUse / UsoExample
Could you tell me + (sujeito + verbo)Pedido educado de informaçãoCould you tell me where it is?
I was wondering if / how...Pergunta suave e indiretaI was wondering how you did it.
May I ask + (palavra interrogativa)Pedir permissão para perguntarMay I ask what method you used?
Ordem: sujeito ANTES do verboSem inversão na 2ª parte: ...where the data is (não "is the data").

Stage 6.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com a palavra correta. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase inteira.

" where the data is from?"
"I was how you reached this figure."
" what method you used?"
"Could you what you mean by fair value?"
"May I ask a quick question?"
"I your clear answer."

Stage 2: Put the Question in Order

Order

Coloque as palavras na ordem correta para formar uma indirect question educada.

?the data
?Could you tell me
?is from?
?where

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e depois grave você mesmo dizendo-a.

May I ask what method you used?
Posso perguntar qual método você usou?
Could you tell me where the data is from?
Você poderia me dizer de onde vêm os dados?
I was wondering how you reached this figure.
Eu estava me perguntando como você chegou a este número.
Could you clarify what you mean by that?
Você poderia esclarecer o que quer dizer com isso?
I appreciate your clear answer, thank you.
Agradeço a sua resposta clara, obrigado.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação numa sessão.

You want to ask the speaker politely about their method. You say:
A "Tell me the method now."
B "May I ask what method you used?"
C "Method what is?"
Which indirect question has the correct word order?
A "Could you tell me where is the report?"
B "Could you tell me where the report is?"
C "Could you tell me where does the report?"
You did not understand a term. You ask:
A "Could you clarify what you mean by fair value?"
B "What? I no understand."
C "Repeat all, please."
The speaker gave a clear answer. You reply:
A "Okay, next."
B "I appreciate your clear answer, thank you."
C "That is wrong."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesmo respondendo à pergunta abaixo. Não há resposta certa ou errada.

Imagine you are in the audience after a talk on valuation. Raise your hand and ask a polite, indirect question (Could you tell me... / I was wondering... / May I ask...), then add a follow-up and ask the speaker to clarify one point. Remember the word order: subject before verb. Speak for one minute.

Survival Card -- Lesson 6

1May I ask what method you used?Posso perguntar qual método você usou?
2Could you tell me where the data is from?Você poderia me dizer de onde vêm os dados?
3I was wondering how you reached this figure.Eu estava me perguntando como você chegou a este número.
4Could you clarify what you mean by that?Você poderia esclarecer o que quer dizer com isso?
5I appreciate your clear answer, thank you.Agradeço a sua resposta clara, obrigado.
Aula 07 -- Pre-class

Answering Questions About Your Work

Clarifying, paraphrasing, and buying time. Key words: rephrase, in other words, buy time, roughly, to be honest, bear with me, touch on, get back to you, put it simply, depend. Structures: time-buying openers + paraphrasing (in other words / to put it simply).
0%
▼

Stage 7.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para escutar.

Rephrase -- reformular (to say the same thing with different words)
"Let me rephrase that more simply."
In other words -- em outras palavras (a phrase to introduce a simpler explanation)
"In other words, we choose the safer value."
Buy time -- ganhar tempo (to gain a few seconds before you answer)
"That is a great question; let me buy a little time."
Roughly -- aproximadamente (not exactly, but about that amount)
"Roughly speaking, it takes two weeks."
To be honest -- para ser honesto (a phrase to introduce a sincere answer)
"To be honest, the answer depends on the market."
Bear with me -- tenha paciência comigo (asking someone to wait a moment)
"Bear with me while I check the figure."
Touch on -- mencionar brevemente (to mention a topic briefly)
"I touched on that point earlier in the talk."
Get back to you -- retornar com a resposta (to answer later, when you have the info)
"Let me check and get back to you on that."
Put it simply -- dizer de forma simples (a phrase to introduce a clear summary)
"To put it simply, we find a fair price."
Depend -- depender (to change according to other conditions)
"It depends on the size of the asset."

Stage 7.2: Matching

Practice

Relacione cada palavra com a definição correta.

Rephrase
In other words
Buy time
Roughly
To be honest
Bear with me
Touch on
Get back to you
Put it simply
Depend

Stage 7.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas.

An investor asks Marcos a hard question. He does not panic. First he buys time: "That is a great question. Let me think." Then he answers, and he rephrases it to be clear: "In other words, we choose the safer value." When she asks for a number he is not sure of, he stays honest: "Roughly two weeks, but let me check and get back to you." He never guesses. "To be honest," he adds, "the exact figure depends on the market." A calm, honest answer builds more trust than a fast, nervous one.

1. Por que Marcos diz "That is a great question. Let me think." primeiro?
A Para mudar de assunto.
B Para ganhar tempo e organizar a resposta.
C Porque não entendeu a pergunta.
2. Qual a preposição correta com "depend"?
A depends of
B depends on
C depends in
3. What does Marcos do when he is not sure of a number?
A He invents an exact figure.
B He gives a rough estimate and offers to get back to her.
C He stays silent.

Stage 7.4: Grammar Tip -- Buying Time & Paraphrasing

GRAMMAR

Como responder com calma e clareza (explicação em inglês e português).

ToolUse / UsoExample
That is a great question / Let me thinkGanhar alguns segundosLet me think for a second.
In other words / To put it simplyParafrasear / esclarecerIn other words, the safer value.
To be honest / Bear with meAbertura honesta / pedir paciênciaBear with me a second.
depend ON (não "of")Use sempre on: It depends on the market.

Stage 7.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com a palavra correta. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase inteira.

"That is a great question. ."
", we choose the safer value."
"To be honest, it on the market."
" two weeks."
"Let me check and on that."
" while I check the figure."

Stage 2: Put the Answer in Order

Order

Coloque os passos de uma boa resposta na ordem correta.

?Paraphrase: "In other words..." or "To put it simply...".
?Buy time: "That is a great question. Let me think."
?Be honest if unsure: "Let me get back to you on that."
?Give your point: answer the question directly.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e depois grave você mesmo dizendo-a.

That is a great question. Let me think.
Essa é uma ótima pergunta. Deixe-me pensar.
In other words, we choose the safer value.
Em outras palavras, escolhemos o valor mais seguro.
To be honest, it depends on the market.
Para ser honesto, depende do mercado.
Bear with me while I check the figure.
Tenha paciência comigo enquanto verifico o número.
Let me check and get back to you on that.
Deixe-me verificar e eu retorno com a resposta.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação sob pressão.

A hard question surprises you. The best first move is:
A Stay silent for ten seconds.
B "That is a great question. Let me think."
C "I do not want to answer."
You do not know the exact figure. You say:
A "It is exactly 14.73%." (a guess)
B "Roughly fifteen percent; let me get back to you with the exact figure."
C "I do not know, sorry."
Your answer is complex. To make it clear, you say:
A "To put it simply, we find a fair price."
B "It is very technical, never mind."
C "You would not understand."
Which sentence is correct?
A "It depends of the size of the asset."
B "It depends on the size of the asset."
C "It depends in the size of the asset."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesmo respondendo à pergunta abaixo. Não há resposta certa ou errada.

Imagine an investor asks you a hard question about your work, and you are not 100% sure of the answer. Respond calmly: buy time with an opener, give your point, paraphrase it ("in other words" / "to put it simply"), and be honest -- offer to get back to them if needed. Speak for one minute.

Survival Card -- Lesson 7

1That is a great question. Let me think.Essa é uma ótima pergunta. Deixe-me pensar.
2In other words, we choose the safer value.Em outras palavras, escolhemos o valor mais seguro.
3To be honest, it depends on the market.Para ser honesto, depende do mercado.
4Bear with me while I check the figure.Tenha paciência comigo enquanto verifico o número.
5Let me check and get back to you on that.Deixe-me verificar e eu retorno com a resposta.
Aula 08 -- Pre-class

Giving a Short Professional Presentation

Structuring a talk with signposting. Key words: outline, overview, agenda, slide, walk you through, highlight, move on to, key takeaway, sum up, wrap up. Structures: signposting (Today I will... / First... / Now let us move on to... / To sum up...).
0%
▼

Stage 8.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para escutar.

Outline -- esboço, roteiro (a short plan of the main points)
"Let me outline what I will cover today."
Overview -- visão geral (a short, general description of the whole)
"First, here is a quick overview of the project."
Agenda -- pauta, agenda (the ordered list of points to cover)
"There are three items on our agenda today."
Slide -- slide (one screen of a presentation)
"On this slide, you can see our results."
Walk you through -- guiar passo a passo (to guide someone step by step)
"Let me walk you through the three main steps."
Highlight -- destacar (to draw attention to the most important part)
"I want to highlight one key result."
Move on to -- passar para (to go from one topic to the next)
"Now let us move on to the results."
Key takeaway -- conclusão principal (the single most important idea)
"The key takeaway is: stay accurate."
Sum up -- resumir (to give a short final summary)
"To sum up, we deliver accurate, reliable reports."
Wrap up -- encerrar, finalizar (to bring a talk to a close)
"Let me wrap up and thank you for your time."

Stage 8.2: Matching

Practice

Relacione cada palavra com a definição correta.

Outline
Overview
Agenda
Slide
Walk you through
Highlight
Move on to
Key takeaway
Sum up
Wrap up

Stage 8.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas.

Marcos gives a short presentation. He opens with a clear signpost: "Today I will give a short overview of our work." Then he sets the structure: "There are three points on the agenda. First, let me walk you through the process." He uses transitions: "Now let us move on to the results." He stresses what matters: "I want to highlight one number." Finally, he closes: "The key takeaway is: accuracy builds trust. To sum up, we deliver reliable reports. Let me wrap up and thank you for your time."

1. O que são "signposting phrases" numa apresentação?
A Frases para encher tempo.
B Frases que guiam a plateia pela estrutura (abertura, ordem, transição, fecho).
C Perguntas para a plateia.
2. Qual frase Marcos usa para FECHAR a apresentação?
A "Today I will..."
B "To sum up... Let me wrap up."
C "First, let me walk you through..."
3. What is the correct phrase to change topic?
A "Let us move to the results."
B "Let us move on to the results."
C "Let us move in the results."

Stage 8.4: Grammar Tip -- Signposting a Presentation

GRAMMAR

Como estruturar uma apresentação (explicação em inglês e português).

Stage / EtapaSignpostExample
Open / AbrirToday I will... / Here is an overviewToday I will show...
Order / OrdenarFirst... / Next... / Finally...First... next... finally...
Transition / TransiçãoNow let us move on to...Move on to the results...
Close / FecharUse To sum up... + key takeaway, depois wrap up: To sum up, accuracy builds trust.

Stage 8.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com a palavra correta. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase inteira.

"Today I will give a short of our work."
"First, let me the process."
"Now let us the results."
"I want to one key result."
"The is: accuracy builds trust."
", thank you for your time."

Stage 2: Put the Presentation in Order

Order

Coloque os estágios de uma apresentação na ordem correta.

?Now let us move on to the results.
?Today I will give a short overview.
?To sum up, let me wrap up and thank you.
?First, let me walk you through the process.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e depois grave você mesmo dizendo-a.

Today I will give a short overview of our work.
Hoje vou dar uma breve visão geral do nosso trabalho.
First, let me walk you through the process.
Primeiro, deixe-me guiá-lo pelo processo.
Now let us move on to the results.
Agora vamos passar para os resultados.
The key takeaway is: accuracy builds trust.
A conclusão principal é: a precisão constrói confiança.
To sum up, thank you for your time.
Para resumir, obrigado pelo seu tempo.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada momento da apresentação.

You start your presentation. The best opener is:
A (start with the first detail, no opening)
B "Today I will give a short overview of our work."
C "I do not know where to start."
You finish one part and start the next. You say:
A "Now let us move on to the results."
B "Let us move to the results."
C "Other thing now."
You want the audience to remember one thing. You say:
A "There are many things."
B "The key takeaway is: accuracy builds trust."
C "I forget the point."
You close the presentation. You say:
A "To sum up, thank you for your time. Let me wrap up."
B "That is all. Bye."
C "I have nothing more."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesmo respondendo à pergunta abaixo. Não há resposta certa ou errada.

Give a short presentation about your work at the congress. Open it ("Today I will..."), present a short agenda, walk the audience through one point, move on to another, highlight the key takeaway, and wrap up ("To sum up..."). Use the signposts. Speak for one to two minutes.

Survival Card -- Lesson 8

1Today I will give a short overview of our work.Hoje vou dar uma breve visão geral do nosso trabalho.
2First, let me walk you through the process.Primeiro, deixe-me guiá-lo pelo processo.
3Now let us move on to the results.Agora vamos passar para os resultados.
4The key takeaway is: accuracy builds trust.A conclusão principal é: a precisão constrói confiança.
5To sum up, thank you for your time.Para resumir, obrigado pelo seu tempo.
Aula 09 -- Pre-class

Trends -- AI in Valuation

Describing trends and changes with present continuous. Key words: trend, emerging, automate, algorithm, data-driven, efficiency, adopt, replace, insight, forecast. Structure: present continuous for change in progress (Technology is changing... / More firms are adopting...).
0%
▼

Stage 9.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para escutar.

Trend -- tendência (a general direction of change over time)
"Automation is the biggest trend in our industry."
Emerging -- emergente (new and starting to grow or appear)
"Artificial intelligence is an emerging technology in valuation."
Automate -- automatizar (to make a task run by machine, not by hand)
"We are starting to automate the slow tasks."
Algorithm -- algoritmo (a set of rules a computer follows to solve a problem)
"The algorithm compares thousands of properties in seconds."
Data-driven -- baseado em dados (guided by data, not by opinion)
"The market is becoming more data-driven every year."
Efficiency -- eficiência (doing a job well with less time or effort)
"These tools are improving our efficiency."
Adopt -- adotar (to start using something new)
"More firms are adopting new software."
Replace -- substituir (to put something new in place of the old)
"AI is not replacing the appraiser; it is supporting us."
Insight -- percepção, análise (a clear, useful understanding of something)
"The data gives us new insight into the market."
Forecast -- previsão, prever (to predict a future value or result)
"The software helps us forecast values more quickly."

Stage 9.2: Matching

Practice

Relacione cada palavra com a definição correta.

Trend
Emerging
Automate
Algorithm
Data-driven
Efficiency
Adopt
Replace
Insight
Forecast

Stage 9.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas.

Marcos describes the trends in his field. "Technology is changing the way we work," he says. "Right now, more firms are adopting artificial intelligence. The tools are automating the slow tasks, so our teams are spending less time on spreadsheets. The market is becoming more data-driven, and clients are asking for faster reports. AI is not replacing the appraiser — it is supporting us. The industry is moving fast, and the firms that are adapting now are gaining an advantage." He uses the present continuous because these changes are happening now, in progress.

1. Por que Marcos usa o present continuous (is/are + -ing)?
A Para falar de hábitos fixos e rotinas.
B Para descrever mudanças e tendências que estão acontecendo agora.
C Para falar do passado.
2. Segundo Marcos, a IA está substituindo o avaliador?
A Sim, está substituindo todos os avaliadores.
B Não — ela está apoiando o avaliador, não substituindo.
C O texto não fala sobre isso.
3. Which sentence is correct present continuous?
A "More firms adopting AI."
B "More firms are adopting AI."
C "More firms is adopt AI."

Stage 9.4: Grammar Tip -- Present Continuous for Trends

GRAMMAR

Como descrever mudanças em progresso (explicação em inglês e português).

Form / FormaStructureExample
Affirmative / Afirmativais / are + verb-ingTechnology is changing our work.
Negative / Negativais / are + not + verb-ingAI is not replacing us.
Question / PerguntaIs / Are + subject + verb-ing?Are firms adopting AI?
When / Quando usarPara mudanças e tendências em progresso agora (use sinais como right now, these days, currently). Não para rotinas fixas (essas usam present simple).

Stage 9.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com a palavra correta. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase inteira.

"Technology is changing our field; the biggest is automation."
"More firms are new software."
"The tools are the slow tasks."
"AI is not the appraiser."
"The market is becoming more ."
"The software helps us values more quickly."

Stage 2: Put the Trend Talk in Order

Order

Coloque as frases de uma fala sobre tendências na ordem correta.

?The tools are automating the slow tasks.
?Technology is changing our industry.
?But AI is not replacing the appraiser.
?More firms are adopting artificial intelligence.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e depois grave você mesmo dizendo-a.

Technology is changing the way we work.
A tecnologia está mudando a forma como trabalhamos.
More firms are adopting artificial intelligence.
Mais empresas estão adotando inteligência artificial.
The tools are automating the slow tasks.
As ferramentas estão automatizando as tarefas lentas.
AI is not replacing us; it is supporting us.
A IA não está nos substituindo; está nos apoiando.
The market is becoming more data-driven.
O mercado está ficando mais baseado em dados.

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação.

A colleague asks what is new in your industry. You say:
A "Nothing change in my field."
B "Technology is changing the way we work."
C "I worked yesterday."
You describe a trend that is happening now. You say:
A "More firms are adopting AI."
B "More firms adopt AI yesterday."
C "More firms is adopting AI."
Someone fears AI will take all the jobs. You reassure them:
A "Yes, the machines replace everyone."
B "AI is not replacing us; it is supporting us."
C "I do not know nothing."
You explain why data matters more now. You say:
A "The market is becoming more data-driven."
B "The market data is."
C "Data is not important."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesmo respondendo à pergunta abaixo. Não há resposta certa ou errada.

Describe two or three trends that are changing your industry right now. Use the present continuous (is/are + -ing) and the new words: trend, emerging, automate, adopt, data-driven, forecast. Say whether AI is replacing people or supporting them, and what you think about it. Speak for one to two minutes.

Survival Card -- Lesson 9

1Technology is changing the way we work.A tecnologia está mudando a forma como trabalhamos.
2More firms are adopting artificial intelligence.Mais empresas estão adotando inteligência artificial.
3The tools are automating the slow tasks.As ferramentas estão automatizando as tarefas lentas.
4AI is not replacing us; it is supporting us.A IA não está nos substituindo; está nos apoiando.
5The market is becoming more data-driven.O mercado está ficando mais baseado em dados.
Aula 10 -- Pre-class

Meetings -- the UPAV Committee

Meeting language and making suggestions. Key words: committee, suggest, propose, schedule, vote, chair, minutes, approve, postpone, attend. Structures: Let's... / Why don't we...? / How about + -ing? / Shall we...? / We could...
0%
▼

Stage 10.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para escutar.

Committee -- comitê, comissão (a group chosen to make decisions)
"The committee meets once a month to make decisions."
Suggest -- sugerir (to offer an idea for others to consider)
"I would like to suggest a new date for the congress."
Propose -- propor (to put forward a plan formally)
"I propose that we open registration earlier."
Schedule -- agendar, cronograma (to plan a time for something)
"Let's schedule the next meeting for Friday."
Vote -- votar, voto (to make a group decision by choosing)
"Shall we put it to a vote?"
Chair -- presidente da reunião (the person who leads a meeting)
"Thank you, Chair, for leading the meeting."
Minutes -- ata (the written record of a meeting)
"Who is taking the minutes today?"
Approve -- aprovar (to officially accept a plan or decision)
"The committee approved the new budget."
Postpone -- adiar (to move something to a later time)
"Can we postpone the vote until next week?"
Attend -- comparecer, participar (to be present at a meeting or event)
"Will you attend the next committee meeting?"

Stage 10.2: Matching

Practice

Relacione cada palavra com a definição correta.

Committee
Suggest
Propose
Schedule
Vote
Chair
Minutes
Approve
Postpone
Attend

Stage 10.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas.

Marcos is in a UPAV committee meeting. The chair opens: "Welcome. Let's start with the agenda." Marcos wants to make an idea heard, so he uses polite suggestions. "I would like to suggest a new date for the congress," he says. "How about holding it in October? Why don't we open registration earlier?" Another member replies: "Good idea. We could also reduce the fee." The chair agrees: "Shall we put it to a vote?" The committee approves the plan, and someone writes it in the minutes.

1. Quando usamos "How about + verbo-ing" ou "Why don't we...?"
A Para dar uma ordem direta.
B Para fazer uma sugestão educada numa reunião.
C Para falar do passado.
2. O que o "chair" faz no fim para decidir?
A Adia a reunião.
B Propõe colocar a ideia em votação ("Shall we put it to a vote?").
C Sai da reunião.
3. Which suggestion is grammatically correct?
A "How about to hold it in October?"
B "How about holding it in October?"
C "How about we holding it in October?"

Stage 10.4: Grammar Tip -- Making Suggestions

GRAMMAR

Como sugerir ideias de forma educada numa reunião (explicação em inglês e português).

Phrase / FraseFollowed by / Seguido deExample
Let's...+ base verbLet's start with the agenda.
Why don't we...?+ base verbWhy don't we open registration earlier?
How about...?+ verb-ingHow about holding it in October?
Shall we...? / We could...+ base verbShall we vote? / We could reduce the fee.
DicaAtenção: How about + verbo-ing (holding), mas Why don't we / Let's / Shall we / We could + verbo base (hold).

Stage 10.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com a palavra correta. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase inteira.

"Let's start; first, the report from last month."
"I would like to a new date for the congress."
"Why don't we the next meeting for Friday?"
"Shall we put it to a ?"
"The committee the new plan."
"Can we the vote until next week?"

Stage 2: Put the Meeting in Order

Order

Coloque as etapas de uma reunião na ordem correta.

?How about holding it in October?
?Let's start with the agenda.
?Shall we put it to a vote?
?I would like to suggest a new date.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e depois grave você mesmo dizendo-a.

Let's start with the agenda.
Vamos começar pela pauta.
I would like to suggest a new date.
Eu gostaria de sugerir uma nova data.
How about holding it in October?
Que tal realizá-lo em outubro?
Why don't we open registration earlier?
Por que não abrimos as inscrições mais cedo?
Shall we put it to a vote?
Vamos colocar em votação?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada momento da reunião.

You open the meeting. You say:
A "Start now the meeting."
B "Let's start with the agenda."
C "I want talk."
You want to suggest a new date politely. You say:
A "How about holding it in October?"
B "How about to hold it in October?"
C "You hold it in October."
You want the group to decide formally. You say:
A "Decide it now."
B "Shall we put it to a vote?"
C "We vote it maybe."
You cannot decide today and need more time. You say:
A "Can we postpone the vote until next week?"
B "We postpone yesterday."
C "No vote forever."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesmo respondendo à pergunta abaixo. Não há resposta certa ou errada.

You are in a UPAV committee meeting. Make two or three suggestions about the next congress (date, venue, registration, or budget). Use Let's... / Why don't we...? / How about + -ing? / Shall we...? / We could... Then close by asking the committee to vote. Speak for one to two minutes.

Survival Card -- Lesson 10

1Let's start with the agenda.Vamos começar pela pauta.
2I would like to suggest a new date.Eu gostaria de sugerir uma nova data.
3How about holding it in October?Que tal realizá-lo em outubro?
4Why don't we open registration earlier?Por que não abrimos as inscrições mais cedo?
5Shall we put it to a vote?Vamos colocar em votação?
Aula 11 -- Pre-class

At the Airport -- Immigration

Travel questions and answers at the airport and immigration. Key words: passport, boarding pass, immigration, customs, declare, baggage, gate, departure, arrival, layover. Structures: travel Q&A (What is the purpose of your visit? / How long are you staying? / I am here for...).
0%
▼

Stage 11.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para escutar.

Passport -- passaporte (the official document for travel)
"May I see your passport, please?"
Boarding pass -- cartão de embarque (the ticket to get on the plane)
"Please have your boarding pass ready."
Immigration -- imigração (passport control when you enter a country)
"All passengers must go through immigration."
Customs -- alfândega (where they check what you bring in)
"Follow the green signs to customs."
Declare -- declarar (to tell officials what you are carrying)
"Do you have anything to declare?"
Baggage -- bagagem (the bags and suitcases you travel with)
"The baggage claim is at carousel five."
Gate -- portão (where you board the plane)
"Boarding starts at gate twenty-two."
Departure -- partida, saída (when a flight leaves)
"The departure is at eight in the evening."
Arrival -- chegada (when a flight reaches its destination)
"Our arrival in Washington is at ten p.m."
Layover -- conexão, escala (a stop between two flights)
"I have a short layover in Miami."

Stage 11.2: Matching

Practice

Relacione cada palavra com a definição correta.

Passport
Boarding pass
Immigration
Customs
Declare
Baggage
Gate
Departure
Arrival
Layover

Stage 11.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas.

Marcos arrives in Washington for the congress. At immigration, an officer asks the usual questions: "May I see your passport, please? What is the purpose of your visit?" Marcos answers clearly: "I am here for a conference." The officer continues: "How long are you staying? Where are you staying? Do you have a return ticket?" Marcos gives short, direct answers: "For five days. At a hotel near the venue. Yes, I do." Finally: "Do you have anything to declare?" Marcos says, "No, nothing to declare." The officer smiles: "Welcome to the United States."

1. Qual é a melhor forma de responder ao oficial de imigração?
A Respostas longas e cheias de detalhes.
B Respostas curtas, claras e diretas.
C Não responder.
2. O que significa "What is the purpose of your visit?"
A "Qual é o seu nome?"
B "Qual é o motivo da sua viagem?"
C "Onde fica o portão?"
3. Which is a correct answer to "How long are you staying?"
A "I am here a conference."
B "For five days."
C "At a hotel."

Stage 11.4: Grammar Tip -- Travel Questions & Answers

GRAMMAR

Perguntas e respostas comuns na imigração (explicação em inglês e português).

Officer's questionMeaning / SignificadoGood answer
What is the purpose of your visit?Motivo da viagem"I am here for a conference."
How long are you staying?Por quanto tempo"For five days."
Where are you staying?Onde vai ficar"At a hotel near the venue."
Do you have anything to declare?Algo a declarar"No, nothing to declare."
DicaRespostas curtas e diretas. I am here for... + motivo; For + tempo; At + lugar.

Stage 11.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com a palavra correta. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase inteira.

"Please have your ready for immigration."
"All passengers must go through ."
"Do you have anything to ?"
"The claim is at carousel five."
"Boarding starts at twenty-two."
"I have a short in Miami."

Stage 2: Put the Immigration Q&A in Order

Order

Coloque as etapas da entrevista de imigração na ordem correta.

?How long are you staying?
?May I see your passport, please?
?Do you have anything to declare?
?What is the purpose of your visit?

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e depois grave você mesmo dizendo-a.

I am here for a conference.
Estou aqui para uma conferência.
I am staying for five days.
Vou ficar por cinco dias.
I am staying at a hotel near the venue.
Vou ficar em um hotel perto do local.
No, I have nothing to declare.
Não, não tenho nada a declarar.
May I see your passport, please?
Posso ver seu passaporte, por favor?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada pergunta da imigração.

The officer asks: "What is the purpose of your visit?" You say:
A "Five days."
B "I am here for a conference."
C "At a hotel."
The officer asks: "How long are you staying?" You say:
A "For five days."
B "For business."
C "Yes, I do."
The officer asks: "Do you have anything to declare?" You say:
A "I am here for a conference."
B "No, nothing to declare."
C "At gate twenty-two."
You want to ask where to collect your bags. You say:
A "Where is the baggage claim?"
B "Where is the conference?"
C "How long are you staying?"

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesmo respondendo à pergunta abaixo. Não há resposta certa ou errada.

Imagine you are at immigration in Washington. Answer the officer's questions out loud: the purpose of your visit, how long you are staying, where you are staying, whether you have a return ticket, and if you have anything to declare. Give short, clear answers. Speak for one to two minutes.

Survival Card -- Lesson 11

1I am here for a conference.Estou aqui para uma conferência.
2I am staying for five days.Vou ficar por cinco dias.
3I am staying at a hotel near the venue.Vou ficar em um hotel perto do local.
4No, I have nothing to declare.Não, não tenho nada a declarar.
5Where is the baggage claim?Onde fica a esteira de bagagem?
Aula 12 -- Pre-class

Hotel Check-in & Getting Help

Checking in and making polite requests at a hotel. Key words: reservation, check-in, receptionist, lobby, key card, amenities, request, complaint, upgrade, vacancy. Structures: polite requests (Could you...? / Can I have...? / Would it be possible to...?).
0%
▼

Stage 12.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para escutar.

Reservation -- reserva (a room booked in advance)
"I have a reservation under the name Mansour."
Check-in -- registro de entrada (when you arrive and get your room)
"Check-in is any time after three p.m."
Receptionist -- recepcionista (the person at the front desk)
"The receptionist gave me my key card."
Lobby -- saguão, recepção (the entrance area of a hotel)
"Breakfast is served in the lobby."
Key card -- cartão-chave (the card that opens your room)
"Here is your key card for room eight-oh-two."
Amenities -- comodidades (the services a hotel offers, like a gym or wifi)
"The hotel amenities include a gym and free wifi."
Request -- pedido, pedir (to politely ask for something)
"I would like to make a request for a quiet room."
Complaint -- reclamação (when you report a problem politely)
"I have a small complaint about the air conditioning."
Upgrade -- melhoria de categoria (a better room than the one booked)
"Could I get an upgrade to a room with a view?"
Vacancy -- vaga, quarto livre (a free room available)
"Do you have any vacancy for tonight?"

Stage 12.2: Matching

Practice

Relacione cada palavra com a definição correta.

Reservation
Check-in
Receptionist
Lobby
Key card
Amenities
Request
Complaint
Upgrade
Vacancy

Stage 12.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas.

Marcos arrives at the hotel and goes to the receptionist. "I have a reservation under the name Mansour," he says. To make his requests polite, he uses can and could: "Could I have a quiet room on a high floor, please? Would it be possible to get a late check-in next time?" Later he has a small complaint: "Could you send someone to look at the air conditioning?" And a question: "Can you tell me the wifi password?" Polite requests with could and would it be possible sound softer and more professional than direct orders.

1. Por que Marcos usa "Could you...?" em vez de uma ordem direta?
A Porque é mais rápido.
B Porque soa mais educado e profissional.
C Porque é o único jeito de pedir.
2. Como Marcos reporta um problema de forma educada?
A "Fix the air conditioning now."
B "Could you send someone to look at the air conditioning?"
C "The room is bad."
3. Which request is the most polite?
A "Give me a quiet room."
B "Could I have a quiet room, please?"
C "I want quiet room."

Stage 12.4: Grammar Tip -- Polite Requests with Can & Could

GRAMMAR

Como pedir de forma educada (explicação em inglês e português).

PhrasePoliteness / EducaçãoExample
Can I / Can you...?Educado (informal)Can you send extra towels?
Could I / Could you...?Mais educadoCould I have a quiet room, please?
Would it be possible to...?Muito educado (formal)Would it be possible to get a late check-out?
DicaDepois de Can/Could use o verbo base (send, have, tell). Adicione please para soar ainda mais educado.

Stage 12.5: Fill in the Blank

Practice

Complete cada frase com a palavra correta. Toque em Listen para ouvir a frase inteira.

"I have a under the name Mansour."
"Could I have my , please?"
"I have a small about the air conditioning."
"Could I get an to a room with a view?"
"Breakfast is served in the ."
"Do you have any for tonight?"

Stage 2: Put the Check-in in Order

Order

Coloque as etapas do check-in na ordem correta.

?Could I have a quiet room, please?
?Good evening. I have a reservation.
?Thank you. Here is my key card.
?It is under the name Mansour.

Stage 3: Pronunciation

Speaking

Ouça cada frase e depois grave você mesmo dizendo-a.

I have a reservation under the name Mansour.
Tenho uma reserva em nome de Mansour.
Could I have a quiet room, please?
Eu poderia ter um quarto silencioso, por favor?
Would it be possible to get a late check-out?
Seria possível conseguir um check-out mais tarde?
Could you send someone to look at the air conditioning?
Você poderia mandar alguém olhar o ar-condicionado?
Can you tell me the wifi password?
Você pode me dizer a senha do wifi?

Stage 4: Situational Quiz

Quiz

Escolha a melhor resposta para cada situação no hotel.

You arrive at the hotel. You say:
A "Give me my room."
B "Good evening. I have a reservation under the name Mansour."
C "Where is the airport?"
You want a quiet room politely. You say:
A "Could I have a quiet room, please?"
B "I want quiet room."
C "Quiet room give me."
The air conditioning is broken. You say:
A "Your hotel is bad."
B "Could you send someone to look at the air conditioning?"
C "Fix it."
You want a very formal, polite request for a late check-out. You say:
A "Would it be possible to get a late check-out?"
B "Late check-out now."
C "I stay more."

Stage 5: Free Production

Reflection

Grave você mesmo respondendo à pergunta abaixo. Não há resposta certa ou errada.

Imagine you are checking in at your Washington hotel. Say you have a reservation, make two or three polite requests (a quiet room, a late check-out, the wifi password), and report one small complaint (for example, the air conditioning). Use Could you...? / Could I have...? / Would it be possible to...? Speak for one to two minutes.

Survival Card -- Lesson 12

1I have a reservation under the name Mansour.Tenho uma reserva em nome de Mansour.
2Could I have a quiet room, please?Eu poderia ter um quarto silencioso, por favor?
3Would it be possible to get a late check-out?Seria possível conseguir um check-out mais tarde?
4Could you send someone to look at the air conditioning?Você poderia mandar alguém olhar o ar-condicionado?
5Can you tell me the wifi password?Você pode me dizer a senha do wifi?
Aula 13 -- Pre-class

Eating Out & Ordering

Ordering food at a restaurant. Key words: menu, appetizer, main course, dessert, beverage, server, bill, tip, recommend, allergy. Structures: making orders (I would like... / I'll have... / Could I get...?).
0%
▼

Stage 13.1: Vocabulary Cards

Vocabulary

Ouça cada palavra e leia o exemplo. Toque em Listen para escutar.

Menu -- cardápio (the list of food and drinks)
"Could I see the menu, please?"
Appetizer -- entrada (a small dish before the main course)
"To start, I would like the soup as an appetizer."
Main course -- prato principal (the largest dish of the meal)
"For the main course, I will have the salmon."
Dessert -- sobremesa (a sweet dish at the end of a meal)
"For dessert, I think I will have the fruit tart."
Beverage -- bebida (a drink)
"Can I get you a beverage while you look at the menu?"
Server -- garçom/garçonete (the person who takes your order)
"The server told us about the specials."
Bill -- conta (the paper that shows what you owe)
"Could we have the bill, please?"
Tip -- gorjeta (extra money for good service)
"In the US, a tip of about twenty percent is normal."
Recommend -- recomendar (to suggest a good dish)
"What do you recommend, the salmon or the chicken?"
Allergy -- alergia (a bad reaction to a certain food)
"I have a mild allergy to nuts."

Stage 13.2: Matching

Practice

Relacione cada palavra com a definição correta.

Menu
Appetizer
Main course
Dessert
Beverage
Server
Bill
Tip
Recommend
Allergy

Stage 13.3: Grammar in Context

GRAMMAR

Leia o texto e responda às perguntas.

After the congress, Marcos has dinner at a restaurant. The server brings the menu and describes the specials. Marcos orders politely. To start, he says: "I would like the tomato soup as an appetizer." For the main course: "I will have the grilled salmon." He makes a small change: "Could I get the vegetables instead of the potatoes?" He also mentions an allergy: "I have a mild allergy to nuts." For dessert: "I think I will have the fruit tart." At the end, he asks: "Could we have the bill, please?" and leaves a tip for good service.

1. Quais frases são usadas para fazer um pedido educado?
A "Give me food."
B "I would like... / I will have... / Could I get...?"
C "Bring everything."
2. Como Marcos pede uma troca no prato?
A "No potatoes."
B "Could I get the vegetables instead of the potatoes?"
C "Change it."
3. Which is the correct way to order the main course?
A "I will having the salmon."
B "I will have the salmon."
C "I would like have the salmon."

Stage 13.4: Grammar Tip -- Making Orders

GRAMMAR

Como fazer um pedido em um restaurante (explicação em inglês e português).

PhraseFollowed by / Seguido deExample
I would like...+ noun (o prato)I would like the soup.
I'll have...+ nounI'll have the salmon.
